title: Proteomic Data Processing and Matrisome Annotation of Human Placenta LC-MS/MS Data
author: Amanpreet K. Bains
materials: Import of Raw Mass Spectrometry Data
Import raw LC-MS/MS files into MaxQuant (version 2.0.3.1).
Specify the digestion enzyme as strict trypsin.
Allow a maximum of two missed cleavage sites.
Use normalized TPI values for downstream comparative analyses.

steps.title: Configure database searching using the uniprotkb_Human_reviewed_2025_03_10 human protein database
steps.instruction: Configure database searching using the uniprotkb_Human_reviewed_2025_03_10 human protein database.

steps.title: Enable MaxQuant LFQ for label-free quantification using default software settings
steps.instruction: Enable MaxQuant LFQ for label-free quantification using default software settings.

steps.title: Set the parent ion mass tolerance to 10 ppm
steps.instruction: Set the parent ion mass tolerance to 10 ppm.

steps.title: Set the fragment ion mass tolerance to 20 ppm
steps.instruction: Set the fragment ion mass tolerance to 20 ppm.

steps.title: Specify carbamidomethylation of cysteine residues as a fixed modification
steps.instruction: Specify carbamidomethylation of cysteine residues as a fixed modification.

steps.title: Specify the following variable modifications
steps.instruction: Specify the following variable modifications: N-terminal glutamine to pyroglutamate conversion (Gln→pyro-Glu) Deamidation of asparagine Deamidation of glutamine Oxidation of methionine Oxidation of proline Oxidation of lysine The latter two variable modifications are included to facilitate detection of extracellular matrix proteins, including collagens and collagen-domain-containing proteins, described in: Characterization of the Extracellular Matrix of Normal and Diseased Tissues Using Proteomics.. Journal of proteome research.

steps.title: Import MaxQuant search results into Scaffold (version 5
steps.instruction: Import MaxQuant search results into Scaffold (version 5.2.2, Proteome Software Inc., Portland, OR) for validation of peptide and protein identifications.

steps.title: Accept peptide identifications only if they...
steps.instruction: Accept peptide identifications only if they achieve a false discovery rate (FDR) below 1.0% by the Percolator posterior error probability calculation. An HMM posterior decoder for sequence feature prediction that includes homology information.. Bioinformatics (Oxford, England).

steps.title: Accept protein identifications only if
steps.instruction: Accept protein identifications only if: Protein FDR is below 1.0% At least two unique peptides are identified per protein

steps.title: Assign protein probabilities using the Protein Prophet algorithm
steps.instruction: Assign protein probabilities using the Protein Prophet algorithm. A statistical model for identifying proteins by tandem mass spectrometry.. Analytical chemistry.

steps.title: Group proteins sharing similar peptides according...
steps.instruction: Group proteins sharing similar peptides according to the principles of parsimony when proteins cannot be distinguished solely by MS/MS evidence.

steps.title: Import validated protein identification data into...
steps.instruction: Import validated protein identification data into Matrisome AnalyzeR, and use this tool to annotate proteins as ECM or non-ECM components. The matrisome: in silico definition and in vivo characterization by proteomics of normal and tumor extracellular matrices.. Molecular & cellular proteomics : MCP. Matrisome AnalyzeR - a suite of tools to annotate and quantify ECM molecules in big datasets across organisms.. Journal of cell science.

steps.title: Classify matrisome proteins into
steps.instruction: Classify matrisome proteins into: Core matrisome components Matrisome-associated components

steps.title: Further classify core matrisome proteins into
steps.instruction: Further classify core matrisome proteins into: ECM glycoproteins Collagens Proteoglycans

steps.title: Further classify matrisome-associated proteins into
steps.instruction: Further classify matrisome-associated proteins into: ECM-affiliated proteins ECM regulators Secreted factors

steps.title: Use total precursor ion intensity (TPI)...
steps.instruction: Use total precursor ion intensity (TPI) values for label-free inter-group quantification and estimation of protein abundance.

steps.title: Calculate a scaling factor for each sample as
steps.instruction: Calculate a scaling factor for each sample as: \mathit{Scaling\ factor} = \frac{\mathit{Total\ TPI\ of\ sample}}{\mathit{Minimum\ total\ TPI\ across\ all\ samples}}

steps.title: Normalize protein TPI values within each...
steps.instruction: Normalize protein TPI values within each sample using the corresponding sample-specific scaling factor to bring all samples to a common intensity level.
